app.title: Agent Session Manager
app.viewUsageTitle: Token & context usage for this session
app.emptyTerminal: Select or create a session to open its terminal.

fork.title: Fork "{{name}}"
fork.noBranch: Continue on the same branch — this session isn't on one
fork.sameBranchHelp: Off: the fork branches off this session's branch, in a worktree of its own. On: it continues on the same branch, in the same directory.
fork.sameBranchHint: The fork shares this session's branch and working directory.
fork.newBranchHint: The fork gets its own branch and working directory, starting from {{branch}}.
fork.liveSameBranchWarning: This session is still running. Two agents editing the same files can overwrite each other's work.
fork.carriesConversation: The fork keeps the same agent, so it carries the whole conversation.
fork.carriesSummary: The fork carries a summary of this session — its requests, the files it changed, and where it left off.

newSession.title: New session
newSession.registeredHint: Registered as a workspace on first use so it stays on the allowlist; remove it later from its workspace node in the sidebar.
newSession.removeWsTitle: Unregister this workspace (files are left intact)
newSession.cleanupTitle: Remove worktrees/branches left in this repo by throwaway or other daemons (that no session here owns)
newSession.confirmCleanup: Scan "{{name}}" for orphaned asm-session worktrees/branches and remove ones no session on this daemon owns?
newSession.forcePrompt: Force-remove the skipped ones too? This DISCARDS uncommitted changes and unmerged commits.
newSession.directCheckout: Run in source checkout instead of an isolated worktree (override)
newSession.autoHint: A fresh <mono>{{prefix}}</mono> branch is created off <mono>{{base}}</mono>.
newSession.existingHint: Checked out in a worktree. If the branch is already open in another session, you'll share its worktree and see the same diffs — concurrent edits from both agents can interfere.
newSession.approveLabel: I approve running this arbitrary command

newWorkspace.title: New workspace on {{daemon}}
newWorkspace.worktreeHint: Sessions started in this workspace run in isolated worktrees when it is a git repository.

directoryPicker.entryTitle: Click to select, double-click to open

sessionList.confirmTakeOver:
This session is open on another client.

Take over? The other client will be disconnected from it.
sessionList.inUseTitle: Open on another client — click to take over
sessionList.forkTitle: Fork — continue this work in a new session, with its context
sessionList.forkUnavailableTitle: Forking needs a coding agent (Claude Code, Codex or opencode) installed on this host
sessionList.archiveTitle: Archive — remove from history and delete branch
sessionList.confirmStop:
Stop "{{name}}"?

The agent is terminated. The session stays in history — you can still read it, save the conversation, or archive it.
sessionList.confirmArchive:
Archive "{{name}}"?

This removes the session from history and deletes the branch and worktree it created. It cannot be undone.
sessionList.confirmArchiveForce:
{{message}}.

Archive anyway and delete the branch? This cannot be undone.
sessionList.missingTitle: {{path}} — no longer exists on the host
sessionList.confirmRemoveWorkspace:
Remove workspace "{{name}}"?

This only unregisters it from this daemon — sessions and files on disk are left intact.
sessionList.disconnectTitle: Disconnect — keep the host listed but stop polling it
sessionList.connectTitle: Connect — resume polling with the same token

branchManager.title: Branches
branchManager.notGit: This workspace is not a git repository.
branchManager.foot: Branches in this repository on {{daemon}}. Actions run on the host.
branchManager.ownedTitle: Created by ASM for a session
branchManager.unmergedTitle: This branch has commits that exist on no other branch — deleting it would lose them
branchManager.staleBaseTitle: The base commit is no longer at the tip of any branch — this branch is built on a stale base
branchManager.deleteBlocked: This branch is checked out in a worktree; close that session first
branchManager.confirmDeleteForce:
{{message}}.

Delete it anyway? This cannot be undone.

vscode.title: Open this session's workspace in VS Code on this machine
vscode.openingRemote: Opening in VS Code via Remote-SSH ({{dest}})…
vscode.didntOpen: VS Code didn't open — it may not be installed on this machine.
vscode.remoteSshHint: Already installed? Remote windows also need the “Remote - SSH” extension and SSH access to {{dest}}.
vscode.cliHint: Or open it from a terminal on this machine:
vscode.relayUnavailable: Not available for relayed hosts yet — VS Code can't reach a node through the relay. A browser-based editor is planned.
vscode.relayUnavailableTitle: Relayed hosts have no direct route from this machine, so VS Code can't open their workspace yet

rightPanel.riskBanner: ⚠ Unsafe session — agent guardrails disabled (skip-permissions / bypass-sandbox)
rightPanel.riskBannerTitle: This session was started with agent guardrails disabled
rightPanel.detachBranchTitle: Detach this session from {{branch}} so another worktree can deploy or verify it
rightPanel.attachBranchTitle: Attach this session back to {{branch}} after the other worktree releases it
rightPanel.detachBranchSuccess: Released {{branch}}. The session kept its current commit and local changes.
rightPanel.attachBranchSuccess: This session is attached to {{branch}} again.
rightPanel.remoteTitle: {{ref}} as of the last fetch. Pull or push to refresh it.
rightPanel.remoteAhead_one: {{count}} commit on this branch that {{ref}} doesn't have
rightPanel.remoteAhead_other: {{count}} commits on this branch that {{ref}} doesn't have
rightPanel.remoteBehind_one: {{count}} commit on {{ref}} that this branch doesn't have
rightPanel.remoteBehind_other: {{count}} commits on {{ref}} that this branch doesn't have
rightPanel.baseRebasedTitle: {{branch}} was last rebased onto this commit.
rightPanel.baseAhead_one: {{count}} commit on this branch since its base
rightPanel.baseAhead_other: {{count}} commits on this branch since its base
rightPanel.notRepo: Not a Git repository. `{{cmd}}` support arrives with workspace isolation.
rightPanel.commitTitle: Ask the session agent to commit the changes
rightPanel.fetchTitle: Fetch every remote (refreshes the commits shown above; changes no branch)
rightPanel.fetchSuccess: Refreshed the remote branches. The commits above are now current.
rightPanel.fetchUpToDate: Every remote was already up to date.
rightPanel.pullTitle: Pull latest into this branch (fast-forward only)
rightPanel.pushTitle: Push this branch to origin (creates it if it doesn't exist)
rightPanel.pushUpToDate: origin is already up to date with {{branch}}.
rightPanel.rebaseUpToDate: {{branch}} is already up to date with {{target}}.
rightPanel.mergeConflictSummary: Conflicts were found. The merge was aborted and both branches were left unchanged.
rightPanel.noOtherBranches: No other branches to rebase onto.
rightPanel.noMergeBranches: No other branches to merge into.

usage.title: Session usage
usage.noData: No usage data available for this session.

commitModal.noFiles: No file changes in this commit.

terminal.takenOver: This session was taken over by another client.

pasteSheet.title: Paste into the terminal
pasteSheet.hint: Long-press here, choose Paste, and it goes straight to the terminal.

api.unreachableAt: cannot connect — daemon unreachable at {{baseUrl}} (not started?)

connection.title: Connections
connection.subtitle: Manage saved hosts, or add a new direct daemon or relay.
connection.currentDaemonsHint: Hosts this client polls for workspaces and sessions.
connection.savedRelaysHint: Relay entries are gateways; nodes discovered through them are nested below.
connection.directTypeDesc: A host you can reach by URL.
connection.relayTypeDesc: A gateway that lists private nodes.
connection.addDirectHint: Use a LAN URL, an SSH tunnel, or an enrolled remote daemon.
connection.addRelayHint: Save the relay first. Its registered nodes will appear under Existing.
connection.sshTip: Tip: for a private host, run <cmd>{{ssh}}</cmd> and add <cmd>{{url}}</cmd> with no token.

relay.empty: No relays yet. Relays don't appear on their own — add one from the Add · Relay tab with its URL and access key. Hosts that dial out to it then show up here, ready to connect.
relay.noNodes: No nodes registered with this relay.
relay.tip: A relay lets this client reach a private host that dials out to it — no tunnels, so it works on any device.
